name: Al Cooper
abstract: Lofton Alfonso Cooper (1911 – October 5, 1981) was an American jazz saxophonist and clarinetist. He founded the Savoy Sultans and was their leader from 1937 to 1946.

about_quotes.speaker: Ernie Fields
about_quotes.evidence: I never did see that record anywhere in the states... He was with me a good while. Al was pretty nice and when he left the band...

about_quotes.speaker: Maxine Sullivan
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Oh, Al Cooper -- I'll tell you, wait a minute, his daughter -- maybe it's his granddaughter won a beauty contest in Cannes, France, a few years ago... Al Cooper now is into real estate in Florida. [INTERVIEWEE] All right!

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions Al Cooper as a hometown boy from Florida who originated the Savoy Sultans, but the interviewee (Cozy Cole) does not respond or comment on this specific individual in the provided text.

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie De Vore
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] You know the Al Cooper, Savoy, Sultans had a New Orleans drummer in them. [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah.

about_quotes.speaker: Carter Benny
about_quotes.evidence: Al Cooper’s Savoy Sultans, which was a little band you always hated to have to follow.
about_quotes.doc_title: Carter Benny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Lew Soloff
about_quotes.evidence: Actually so was Al Cooper, before I was there. I loved his songs.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lew Soloff interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, April 16, 2000
